reference: Hypertension is a condition in which blood pressure remains consistently higher than normal.
response: Hypertension is high blood pressure, which puts a strain on the heart and arteries. Over time, it can damage blood vessels, potentially leading to stroke, heart failure, or kidney failure.

reference: Pneumonia is commonly caused by bacterial viral or fungal infections that inflame the air sacs in the lungs.
response: Pneumonia can result from a severe infection in the area of the lung served by an affected bronchial tube. It can also be caused by bacteria (common pneumonia), tuberculosis, fungi, parasites, or other germs.
